name: Creamy Avena
intro: Avena is very nostalgic to me. It is oatmeal that’s smoother and sweeter. This is made with evaporated coconut milk, cinnamon, nutmeg, vanilla extract and love. Because that’s how I remember it tasting when I used to get it from the Hispanic bakery up the block from where I lived in Castle Hill. This was years ago, and to my surprise, I saw it is still in business. Clearly for a very good reason! This avena is so smooth, you could drink it out of the cup. It is perfect on a cold day in New York.
method: In a blender, combine the water and 1 cup (80 g) of the oats and blend to get a liquid with small pieces. Do not overblend.

In a medium-sized pot over high heat, combine the evaporated coconut milk, oat milk, cinnamon sticks, ground cinnamon, cloves, salt and nutmeg. Add the blended mixture and the remaining ½ cup (40 g) of oats to the pot, along with the vanilla and coconut sugar. Lower the heat to a simmer and cook, stirring constantly, for 7 minutes. When the oats are soft, the avena is ready to serve.
